articles.title: A Dormant Dream Erupts: 18th-Century Mechanical Volcano Built After 250 Years
articles.summary: A clockwork volcano designed in 1775 to mimic Mount Vesuvius has been constructed for the first time. Engineering students used modern tech like programmable LEDs to realize the vision. The exhibit runs at the University of Melbourne through June 28, 2026.

articles.title: Brain Scans Reveal Why Number Symbols Trip Up Kids with Math Disabilities
articles.summary: A new study shows that kids with math learning disabilities process number symbols differently in the brain. When problems use dots instead, their brain activity and behavior match typical peers. The findings point to specific brain regions involved.

articles.title: In a Real-World ER Test, an AI Model Outperformed Doctors at Diagnosis
articles.summary: A study in Science found that an OpenAI reasoning model beat two experienced physicians in diagnosing emergency department patients. The AI used only text from electronic health records and correctly identified a missed lupus case.
